Title: Root URL landing page returns welcome message
Preconditions: Server is running on configured PORT
Steps: 1. Send GET request to / | 2. Verify response status | 3. Check response structure
Expected Result: Response status 200 | JSON contains "message": "Welcome to Oktawave Loan Origination System" | Contains endpoints object with landing, auth, health | Contains callToAction with "Start Your Loan Application" text and /api/landing link

Title: Landing page displays complete content with Oktawave branding
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing | 2. Verify branding colors in response | 3. Check sections array | 4. Validate call-to-action buttons
Expected Result: Response contains branding.colors with primary: #003366, secondary: #0066CC, accent: #FF6600 | Typography includes Open Sans and Roboto fonts | Sections include hero, features, how-it-works, cta-secondary | Multiple CTAs present with "Apply Now" primary button

Title: Complete user journey from root to loan application
Preconditions: Server is running and all routes are configured
Steps: 1. Access root URL / | 2. Parse callToAction.link from response | 3. Navigate to /api/landing | 4. Locate primary CTA button | 5. Extract /api/loan/apply link | 6. Verify end-to-end flow structure
Expected Result: Root returns callToAction pointing to /api/landing | Landing page loads with complete branding | Hero section contains "Start Your Application" CTA with /api/loan/apply link | Secondary CTA section has "Apply Now" button also pointing to /api/loan/apply | User journey clearly guides toward loan application

Title: Retrieve specific landing page sections via content API
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing/content/branding | 2. Send GET request to /api/landing/content/testimonials | 3. Send GET request to /api/landing/content/faq | 4. Verify each response structure
Expected Result: Branding section returns colors, typography, and logo paths | Testimonials returns array with rating, name, role, text | FAQ returns array with question/answer pairs | All responses return 200 status

Title: Landing page features section displays correctly
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing | 2. Extract sections array | 3. Find section with type: 'features' | 4. Verify items array
Expected Result: Features section contains heading "Why Choose Oktawave?" | Items array has 4 features: Quick Approval, Secure & Safe, Transparent Terms, 24/7 Support | Each item has icon, title, description fields

Title: How It Works process section displays all steps
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing | 2. Locate section with type: 'process' | 3. Validate steps array
Expected Result: Process section contains 4 steps in sequence | Step 1: Create Account | Step 2: Complete Application | Step 3: Get Approved | Step 4: Receive Funds | Each step has step number, title, and description

Title: Footer contains company information and links
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing | 2. Extract footer object | 3. Verify all footer properties
Expected Result: Footer contains companyName: "Oktawave Financial Services" | Tagline: "Empowering your financial future" | Links array includes About Us, Contact, Terms & Conditions, Privacy Policy | Copyright includes current year 2026

Title: Request non-existent content section returns 404
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing/content/nonexistent | 2. Verify response status | 3. Check error message
Expected Result: Response status 404 | JSON contains {"message": "Section not found"}

Title: Landing page timestamp and apiVersion are dynamically generated
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing | 2. Extract meta.timestamp | 3. Verify timestamp is valid ISO string | 4. Check apiVersion
Expected Result: meta.timestamp is valid ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2026-05-07T05:21:54.123Z") | meta.apiVersion is "1.0.0" | Timestamp is current server time

Title: CORS headers allow configured frontend origin
Preconditions: Server running with FRONTEND_URL env variable set
Steps: 1. Send OPTIONS preflight request to / with Origin header | 2. Verify CORS headers in response | 3. Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin
Expected Result: Access-Control-Allow-Origin matches configured FRONTEND_URL | Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is true | CORS middleware properly configured

Title: Invalid route returns 404 error
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/invalid-route | 2. Verify response status | 3. Check error message
Expected Result: Response status 404 | JSON contains {"message": "Route not found"}

Title: Malformed content section request returns 404
Steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/landing/content/ (empty section) | 2. Verify response
Expected Result: Returns 404 or redirects appropriately | Does not cause server error
